hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific complementarity with ECL2/TM5 while reducing off-target affinity, increasing selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are not sufficiently disrupting conserved off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions. Targeted mutations at positions 1, 2, 5, 6, 11, and 12 are needed to improve selectivity over native SST-14.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are known to contribute to off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing design on these positions can help disrupt conserved-pocket contacts while preserving key pharmacophore residues.
Introducing polar or hydrophobic mutations at these positions can disrupt conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5, particularly those involving hydrophobic or hydrogen-bonding motifs.
